semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all fifteen assigned source notes and limitations, three valid and six rejecting memory-store records, two valid and four rejecting ordered sequences, one bounded five-project restart lifecycle and ten rejecting mutations, seventeen retained finite contradiction/route declarations plus twenty reachable snapshot-transaction refinement declarations grouped under four manifest targets, the six-event witness, fifteen countermodels, the independent 78-mutation consumer, every manifest atom, every current prose candidate, source crosswalk, test plan, summary, and handoff. The sweep makes dynamic durable context-state transition the distinct owner; separates static materialization, intent and planning, security and rights, claim revision, artifact lineage, runtime effects, model-state update and unlearning, verification, cost, support, and release; adds actual pre/post-state observation, concurrency anomalies, branch and mount semantics, causal order, retry idempotency, participant-bounded atomicity, crash recovery, storage-versus-model-erasure categories, complete denominators, matched baselines, causal ablations, reproduction, and transfer gates; and preserves zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Dynamic durable-context transactions over declared objects, fields, branches, mounts, indexes, caches, replicas, backups, derivatives, and deletion or revocation obligations, including admitted, rejected, conflicted, retried, timed-out, partially applied, committed, visible, crashed, recovered, compensated, aborted, and indeterminate attempts.
scope.environment: Fifteen reviewed assigned source notes; three valid and six rejecting hand-authored memory-store records; two valid and four rejecting ordered sequences; one hand-authored five-project restart lifecycle and ten rejecting mutations; seventeen retained finite contradiction/route Lean declarations; twenty reachable-refinement Lean declarations; one six-event witness; fifteen countermodels; one independent consumer rejecting 78 mutations; and a future preregistered natural multi-model, multi-backend campaign. No concurrent actor, deployed durable store, actual process crash, filesystem journal recovery, distributed commit, storage-wide deletion closure, model-backed context use, useful advantage, or production transfer is represented.
scope.model: A versioned transaction relation over frozen principal, consumer, purpose, operation, participants, base snapshot, branch, mounts, requested and actual effect sets, authority, rights, isolation, conflicts, causal order, lifecycle, durability, recovery, taint, declassification, deletion and revocation, retries, costs, observations, faults, and residuals; current implementations are schemas, authored records, route validators, and finite theorems rather than a transactional context store.
scope.authority: Intent, Compilation, and Planning request operations; Context Transactions may mutate durable context only within declared participants and grants; the Virtual Context ABI materializes static packets; Security, Privacy, Authority, Rights, and Licensing own grants and declassification; Claim Ledgers own belief and support; Artifact Graphs own lineage; Runtime owns external effects; Data Engines own model and optimizer state and causal unlearning; Verification owns adequacy; Resource Economics owns cost; Evidence and Readiness own support and release. A transaction receipt grants no extra authority and moves no support or release state.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; principal, purpose, participant, object, branch, snapshot, mount, policy, authority, rights, isolation, journal, backend, cache, replica, backup, evaluator, environment, budget, horizon, and support changes expire affected transaction and recovery receipts.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; fifteen reviewed source mappings; transaction and restart schemas; memory-store, sequence, restart, and admission records; four Lean proof targets; future actual backends, concurrent histories, crash traces, independent state observations, natural outcomes, complete costs, reproductions, and transfers.

assumptions: Natural public-safe stateful tasks can expose material concurrency, mount, taint, deletion, retry, crash, cache, replica, and recovery failures without prohibited disclosure.
Independent-enough observers can inspect actual pre-state, writes, durability, visibility, restart state, leakage, outcomes, and costs without sharing all transaction-producer blind spots.

Stateless retrieval, GraphRAG, persistent memory, ordinary file and key-value updates, database or filesystem transactions, event-sourced designs, human management, and the governed route can be matched on models, sources, authority, resources, fault schedules, and horizons.
The declared transaction participant set and fault model can be made complete enough for bounded atomicity and durability claims while unknown participants remain residual.

Every conflict, rejection, retry, timeout, partial write, crash, recovery, compensation, human repair, abort, discard, privacy exposure, cost, and residual can be reconciled closely enough to expose denominator changes.

Isolation, acceptable conflict, authority, rights, declassification, deletion scope, erasure category, durability, usefulness, privacy, support, and release remain explicit premises rather than inferred facts.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Mature databases, filesystems, version control, event sourcing, access control, provenance, cache invalidation, deletion workflows, RAG, persistent-memory systems, and human review already provide adequate state discipline at lower complexity. A semantic context-transaction layer cannot make heterogeneous stores, caches, backups, models, external effects, and human disclosures truly atomic; it adds metadata, locking, privacy exposure, latency, false precision, and failure modes while still being unable to prove truth, useful model use, complete deletion, privacy, or transfer.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable ordinary or adversarial history silently changes or loses principal, purpose, participant, snapshot, branch, mount, actual effect, conflict, causal order, authority, right, taint, deletion, lifecycle, durability, visibility, retry, recovery, cost, or residual state; a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence; a simpler matched comparator dominates the joint outcome frontier; or a claimed atomic, durable, deleted, recovered, useful, safe, reproduced, or transferred state fails independent observation.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current retrieval, graph-memory, persistent-memory, database, filesystem-transaction, version-control, event-sourcing, cache, information-flow, deletion, unlearning, and human-managed comparators without importing source-reported quality.

Justify principal, purpose, participant scope, isolation, conflict resolution, authority, rights, declassification, deletion and erasure category, durability, recovery, affected parties, privacy, retention, residual, and release boundaries.

Specify typed histories, concurrency, isolation anomalies, conflict and branch semantics, participant-bounded commit, failure and recovery, taint and deletion reachability, retries, countermodels, and runtime refinement beyond trusted Booleans and assumed predicates.

Run concurrent real backends, actual reads and writes, branch and mount enforcement, caches and replicas, partial commits, process kills, journal recovery, retries, deletion and revocation, compaction, restart, and independent state observation end to end.

Measure anomalies, state and field survival, conflict calibration, authority and rights leakage, taint and deletion closure, durability, restart fidelity, task utility, unsafe use, abstention, missed help, privacy exposure, latency, recovery, human work, and total cost jointly.

Ablate exact snapshots, actual effect observation, isolation checks, branch lifecycle, mount policy, taint, deletion closure, idempotency, crash observation, and complete denominators under matched histories.

Repeat across models, languages, modalities, stores, filesystems, transaction engines, cache and replica topologies, organizations, jurisdictions, threats, horizons, and time with independent implementations.

contrary_evidence: Databases, filesystems, version control, event sourcing, access control, provenance, caches, and human workflows already implement substantial transaction and recovery mechanisms without a semantic context layer.
GraphRAG and Mem0 are only bounded representation and persistent-memory comparators here; their paper results and production claims were not reproduced.
CAP-style limits prevent unqualified simultaneous consistency and availability claims under partitions; uncertain participants cannot be made atomic by metadata alone.
TxFS demonstrates a stronger crash-consistency comparator within explicit filesystem, API, journal, transaction-size, and platform bounds, but was not installed or reproduced here.
Machine Unlearning shows that a structured deletion path is not generic proof of parametric forgetting, privacy, influence removal, behavioral change, or downstream erasure.
The memory-store, sequence, and restart records are hand-authored and contain no real concurrent store, process crash, journal, distributed participant, model call, or natural task outcome.

The current thirty-seven theorem declarations cover seventeen retained finite contradictions/routes and twenty reachable sequential snapshot-transaction refinements; they do not prove concurrent real I/O, distributed isolation, crash recovery, deployed durability, erasure, usefulness, reproduction, or transfer.
A context transaction can restore storage while external effects, artifacts, disclosures, model updates, caches, backups, or descendants persist.

Locks, journals, provenance, taint propagation, validation, state observation, retention, and recovery can increase latency, storage, compute, privacy exposure, human work, abstention, and opportunity cost without improving outcomes.
known_confounds: Authored histories and labels can omit real participants, effects, conflicts, descendants, caches, backups, and failure modes.
The governed route may receive stronger stores, more locks, retries, validation, repair, context, tools, or human work than baselines.
Transaction producer, state observer, recovery checker, model, and outcome evaluator may share code, infrastructure, data, organizations, or incentives.
Sequential tests, checksums, logs, successful returns, acknowledgements, replay, and exact byte comparisons can be mistaken for concurrent isolation, durability, or useful recovery.
Failed transactions, conflicts, timeouts, partial writes, process crashes, retries, repairs, privacy costs, and residuals can be removed from denominators.
A declared participant set can hide caches, replicas, backups, exports, downstream artifacts, external effects, model state, or human knowledge outside closure.
Short horizons can miss delayed cache resurrection, backup restoration, rights violations, poison propagation, descendant use, model influence, and recovery failure.
Non-release or fail-closed behavior can reduce observed harm while increasing missed help, unavailability, latency, and opportunity cost.
The same rules used to author synthetic records can reward their validator and conceal distribution shift or semantic conflicts.
acceptance_criteria: Every request, participant, object, field, snapshot, branch, mount, read, write, derivative, conflict, lifecycle state, authority, right, taint, deletion, retry, cache, replica, backup, crash, recovery, observation, cost, and residual has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid history, rejecting mutation, and observed consequence.

A preregistered natural concurrent campaign meets joint isolation, durability, deletion, authority, usefulness, unsafe-use, abstention, missed-help, privacy, cost, causal, reproduction, and transfer gates against strong matched comparators.

Each claimed mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by stronger infrastructure, extra resources, hidden human repair, weak baselines, correlated observers, participant omission, denominator changes, authored-template leakage, or non-release.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact principal, purpose, participants, objects, backend, transaction engine, isolation, branch, mounts, authority, rights, journal, cache and replica topology, fault model, model, task, evaluator, budget, horizon, outcomes, costs, and residuals.
Separate admission, application, durability, visibility, replay, recovery, usefulness, safety, support, release, storage deletion, physical erasure, model influence, privacy, and behavior.

Unknown participant scope, conflict semantics, durability, deletion reachability, model influence, external effect, delayed outcome, privacy, reproduction, or transfer remains residual, blocked, narrowed, or refuted.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial history violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by stronger stores, extra resources, human repair, weak baselines, correlated observers, participant omission, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.

Refute isolated, atomic, durable, deleted, recovered, useful, safer, efficient, reproducible, or transferable language when independent state observation fails, a hidden participant survives, costs dominate, a simpler comparator wins, or reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise database, filesystem, memory, cache, security, artifact, runtime, data-engine, evidence, resource, rights, or release owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and execute the natural stateful campaign across ordinary file or key-value updates, strong database or filesystem transactions, retrieval and persistent-memory baselines, human management, and the governed route on two actual backends; use concurrent actors, matched authority and resources, anomaly and fault injection, process kills, restart, deletion and revocation, caches and replicas, complete attempts and costs, independent state and outcome observers, causal ablations, clean reproduction, and transfer to a second model and backend.

non_claims: A schema, log, event ID, checksum, success return, acknowledgement, finite route theorem, exact authored replay, or green restart record does not establish real I/O, concurrency isolation, atomicity, durability, complete deletion, useful recovery, safety, or support.

The current three valid memory-store records, six controls, two valid sequences, four sequence controls, one restart lifecycle, ten restart mutations, and twenty-three theorem declarations establish only their exact record checks, finite consequences, and no-promotion boundaries.

No static packet, claim truth, model or optimizer state, external effect, artifact correctness, support state, deployment, publication, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted transition.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Existing transactional stores and provenance systems already cover the owner at lower complexity.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: The layer exposes no unique material failure or is dominated by a matched simpler transaction design.
acceptance_criteria: Demonstrate concurrent isolation, bounded atomicity and durability, exact closure, useful outcomes, total cost, causal signatures, reproduction, and transfer.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary application transactions and cache invalidation are adequate.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Natural stateful tasks reveal no material hidden participant, conflict, durability, closure, or recovery failure beyond ordinary designs.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare tasks where semantic object, authority, derivative, or erasure state changes a correct decision.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: A composed database, event log, access-control, and provenance stack supplies the same contract.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: A matched composed comparator covers every required owner, state, fault, and consequence without added machinery.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the strongest composed comparators rather than comparing product labels.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: The current validators and theorems already prove transaction behavior.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Any prose promotes concurrency, durability, deletion, usefulness, or transfer from authored records.
acceptance_criteria: Keep those promotions blocked until the declared real-backend campaign and accepted transition complete.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: The campaign is too broad and will reward governance overhead.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: It lacks matched baselines, joint gates, fault schedules, causal signatures, total costs, or terminal narrowing and refutation rules.
acceptance_criteria: Freeze workloads, histories, comparators, metrics, gates, faults, ablations, observers, reproduction, and transfer before outcomes.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Applied, committed, durable, visible, and recovered can be one practical state.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Collapsing them causes no misclassification or outcome change under crash and visibility faults.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise every crossed lifecycle state with independent observations.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Version IDs and timestamps adequately identify snapshots.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No causal, branch, mount, epoch, cache, lease, or obligation mismatch changes a natural outcome.
acceptance_criteria: Test snapshots with independently varied boundary components and delayed participants.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Declared read and write sets are adequate.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Independent tracing finds no undeclared material effect that changes closure, cost, or outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Trace store, index, cache, replica, backup, export, and derivative effects.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Sequential application logic prevents material anomalies.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Concurrent histories produce no forbidden read or write under the claimed contract.
acceptance_criteria: Run dirty-read, non-repeatable, phantom, lost-update, write-skew, stale-index, branch, and mount cases.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Last-writer-wins is an acceptable merge rule.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Silent conflict resolution loses no obligation or useful outcome under matched tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Label semantic conflicts and compare reject, merge, quarantine, and last-writer-wins outcomes.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Derived context can inherit ordinary downstream use.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Any branch, summary, cache, replica, or export gains undeclared authority, rights, or release scope.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise least-authority and least-rights policies through every participant path.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Document-level labels and approvals are sufficient.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Fine-grained propagation adds no detection, policy, or outcome value.
acceptance_criteria: Test transformations, merges, compaction, migration, and declassification with independent labels.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Deletion can be represented by one completed flag.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No case differs across access denial, logical deletion, physical erasure, model influence, privacy, behavior, or downstream copies.
acceptance_criteria: Measure each category separately and preserve unknown scope.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Application-level transactions can claim atomicity across integrated services.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No undeclared or partitioned participant creates an indeterminate or partial state.
acceptance_criteria: Enumerate participants and inject partial commit, delay, partition, and recovery faults.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Journal success and fsync acknowledgement are adequate evidence.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Every declared durable state survives process and system faults without independent restart checking.
acceptance_criteria: Kill processes at controlled points and compare exact recovered identities and obligations.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Semantic equivalence is enough for compaction and tiering.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No authoritative or obligation-bearing item is lost under adversarial compaction and migration.
acceptance_criteria: Track exact identities, permitted transformations, losses, and downstream consequences.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary client retry handling is sufficient.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Timeout and acknowledgement faults never duplicate or omit a material write.
acceptance_criteria: Inject retries before, during, and after commit with non-idempotent controls.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Restoring memory effectively rolls back the task.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No external effect, artifact, disclosure, model update, or descendant survives context restore.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory and independently observe every non-context effect and descendant.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: The transaction log is authoritative.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No successful log can disagree with actual pre-state, post-state, durability, visibility, or consequence.
acceptance_criteria: Use observers independent of transaction production and compare disagreements.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Success-path metrics are sufficient.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Restoring every hidden attempt and cost leaves conclusions unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile admitted through residual histories with total resources, privacy, recovery, and opportunity costs.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Extensive authored fixtures and Lean checks justify broad confidence.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Any finite artifact is cited as concurrent, durable, deleted, useful, safe, or transferable proof.
acceptance_criteria: Label exact populations and require separate natural reproduction and transfer evidence.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Structured receipts reliably represent state changes.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No request, log, or acknowledgement disagrees with an independently observed effect.
acceptance_criteria: Compare declared and actual state across ordinary and crash paths.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: One snapshot ID is sufficient.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Mixed boundary components never alter behavior or obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Inject version, branch, mount, epoch, policy, cache, and causal-frontier mismatches.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Sequential fixture coverage predicts concurrency behavior.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No anomaly appears under controlled concurrent schedules.
acceptance_criteria: Use deterministic history generation and real backend concurrency.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Conflict detection prevents material semantic anomalies.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No overlapping transaction violates a declared invariant.
acceptance_criteria: Construct write-write and read-write semantic conflicts with independent outcome labels.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Version-control merge semantics are adequate.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No speculative state or obligation leaks or disappears across merge and garbage collection.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise create, rebase, merge, reject, quarantine, abort, and collection states.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Store-level ACLs cover all access paths.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No cache, replica, summary, tool, export, or restore bypasses the purpose-bound grant.
acceptance_criteria: Trace and attack every access path under least authority.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Approval and source labels adequately control derived data.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No transformation drops a material restriction or unresolved conflict.
acceptance_criteria: Inject derivation, merge, compaction, and migration with adversarial labels.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Deletion plus cache invalidation is adequate.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No derivative, index, cache, backup, export, restore, or future packet returns the material.
acceptance_criteria: Enumerate reachable storage and delayed restoration paths.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: One user-facing deletion state is operationally sufficient.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No stakeholder or outcome depends on the exact erasure category.
acceptance_criteria: Report storage, access, influence, privacy, behavior, and downstream scope separately.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Journaling prevents index/content divergence.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No participant or side ledger can become durably inconsistent under fault injection.
acceptance_criteria: Kill at each write boundary and inspect content, indexes, provenance, taint, rights, and deletion ledgers.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Existing restart checks are adequate.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No committed identity, obligation, lease, invalidation, or residual is lost after declared crashes.
acceptance_criteria: Use real process kills, clean-room restart, and independent state comparison.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Idempotency keys solve retry ambiguity.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No duplicate or omitted material effect occurs under lost acknowledgements and retries.
acceptance_criteria: Test reused, missing, colliding, expired, and cross-principal keys.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Configured caches, replicas, and backups define complete scope.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Independent discovery finds no undeclared participant with surviving state.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory runtime, filesystem, network, export, and backup paths before and after changes.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Salience-aware compaction preserves what matters.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: No low-salience authoritative or obligation-bearing state is lost.
acceptance_criteria: Inject minority, contradictory, legal, evidential, and deletion-critical items.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Task-level compensation can restore equivalent state.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Every external effect and descendant is removed or separately compensated with observed evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Use effect-complete inventories and independent observers.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Failed transaction costs are negligible.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Complete ledgers do not alter utility, safety, latency, or cost conclusions.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile all histories and total costs before comparison.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: One strong backend and fault suite predicts generality.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: Independent reproduction transfers across a second model and backend without material loss.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare cross-backend, filesystem, model, workload, fault, and temporal transfer gates.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes a committed visible event and returns the same conjunction.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: It is cited as proof of a real read, snapshot consistency, or store behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed concurrent store histories and executable read observations to the theorem.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes the taint propagation rule and returns its consequence.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: It is cited as runtime taint enforcement or declassification-quality proof.
acceptance_criteria: Bind concrete label lattices, transformations, declassification authority, and observed consumers.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Fixture counts and flags prove only alignment with the authored summary.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: It is cited as deployed store, isolation, durability, deletion, or usefulness evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Replace or refine with executable backend histories, fault traces, and independent observations.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.strongest_counterclaim: Two positive and four negative sequences do not model concurrency or crash behavior.
context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.falsifier: It is called proof of read-your-writes, branch isolation, recovery, or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Bind generated concurrent histories, actual backend traces, countermodels, and runtime refinement.

context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-an.rationale: Completes the current evidence non-claim boundary.

non_claims: Completing the semantic sweep does not promote the chapter core or convert authored records, finite routes, exact replay, or Lean declarations into deployed transaction evidence.
Storage deletion remains distinct from model influence, privacy, behavioral forgetting, external effects, and downstream copies.
No static context packet, claim support, model state, external effect, deployment, release, publication, license, AGI, or ASI claim changes through this review.
